en: Conclusion. —The really interesting and valuable séances came to an end with the production of the system of powers. Before this a gradual decline in the vividness of the ecstasies was noticeable. Ulrich von Gerbenstein came increasingly to the front, and filled up the séances with his childish chatter. The visions which S. W. had in the meantime likewise seem to have lost vividness and plasticity of formation, for S. W. was afterwards only able to feel pleasant sensations in the presence of good spirits, and disagreeableness in that of bad spirits. Nothing new was produced. There was something of uncertainty in the trance talks, as if feeling and seeking for the impression which she was making upon the audience, together with an increasing staleness in the content. In the outward behaviour of S. W. there arose also a marked shyness and uncertainty, so that the impression of wilful deception became ever stronger. The writer therefore soon withdrew from the séances. S. W. experimented afterwards in other circles, and six months after my leaving was caught cheating in flagranti delicto . She wanted to arouse again by spiritualistic experiments the lost belief in her supernatural powers; she concealed small objects in her dress, throwing them up in the air during the dark séance. With this her part was played out. Since then, eighteen months have passed during which I have not seen S. W. I have learnt from an observer who knew her in the earlier days, that she has now and again strange states of short duration during which she is very pale and silent, and has a fixed glittering look. I did not hear any more of visions. She is said not to take part any longer in spiritualistic séances. S. W. is now in a large business, and according to all accounts is an industrious and responsible person who does her work eagerly and cleverly, giving entire satisfaction. According to the account of trustworthy persons, her character has much improved; she has become quieter, more regular and sympathetic. No other abnormalities have appeared in her. This case, in spite of its incompleteness, contains a mass of psychological problems whose exposition goes far beyond the limits of this little work. We must therefore be satisfied with a mere sketch of the various striking manifestations. For the sake of a more lucid exposition it seems better to review the various states separately.
zh: 結論。——隨著「能力系統」的產生，那些真正有趣且有價值的降神會便告終結。在此之前，可以注意到出神狀態的生動程度已逐漸衰退。烏爾裡希·馮·格本斯坦越來越常出頭，並以他幼稚的閒談填滿了降神會。與此同時，S. W. 所見的異象似乎也失去了生動性與形塑的立體感，因為後來 S. W. 只能在善靈面前感受到愉悅的感覺，在惡靈面前則感到不適。沒有新的東西被產生出來。在出神談話中出現了一種不確定性，彷彿她在感受並尋求自己對聽眾所留下的印象，同時內容也變得越來越陳腐。在 S. W. 的外在行為上，也出現了明顯的羞怯與不確定，以至於蓄意欺騙的印象變得越來越強烈。因此，筆者不久便退出了降神會。S. W. 後來在其他圈子中繼續實驗，並在我離開六個月後，被當場抓獲作弊。她想要藉由靈性實驗來重新喚起人們對她超自然能力已失去的信任；她將小物件藏在衣服裡，在黑暗的降神會中將它們拋向空中。至此，她的角色便告終結。自那時起，已過去十八個月，期間我未曾見過 S. W.。我從一位早期認識她的觀察者那裡得知，她偶爾會出現短暫的奇怪狀態，在此期間她臉色蒼白、沉默不語，並帶有呆滯而閃爍的目光。我沒有再聽說任何異象。據說她不再參加任何靈性降神會。S. W. 現在在一家大企業工作，根據所有說法，她是一個勤奮且負責任的人，熱情而聰明地完成工作，令人完全滿意。根據可信人士的描述，她的性格大有改善；她變得更加安靜、規律且富有同情心。沒有其他異常現象出現在她身上。這個案例儘管不完整，卻包含了大量的心理學問題，其闡述遠遠超出了這本小書的範圍。因此，我們只能滿足於對各種顯著現象的簡要勾勒。為了更清晰的闡述，似乎最好分別回顧各種狀態。
